title: centaurea avilae
intro: Centaurea avilae, also known as Avila knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Centaurea avilae can be consumed as a tea, used as a tincture, or incorporated into creams and salves for topical application.
